root_nodes.description: Implements the fundamental cJSON library: JSON parsing from text to cJSON structures, serialization from cJSON structures to text, memory management via configurable hooks, and creation/manipulation of JSON types (null, boolean, number, string, array, object).

Implements advanced JSON operations building on the core library: JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) navigation, JSON Patch (RFC 6902) application and generation, JSON Merge Patch (RFC 7386) application and generation, and object sorting.
Contains demonstration code and fuzzing harnesses (libFuzzer and AFL) that exercise the cJSON library by parsing JSON input and testing serialization functions.

intermediate_nodes.description: Implements all core cJSON functionality: parsing (parse_value, parse_array, parse_object, parse_string, parse_number), serialization (print_value, print_array, print_object, print_string, print_number), memory management (cJSON_New_Item, cJSON_Delete, cJSON_strdup), type creation (cJSON_CreateNull/True/False/Bool/Number/String/Array/Object/Raw), array/object manipulation (add/insert/replace/detach/delete items), comparison (cJSON_Compare), minification (cJSON_Minify), and public API wrappers (cJSON_Parse, cJSON_Print, etc.). Manages static global state: global_error, version, global_hooks, default_buffer_size.
Declares the public API for the cJSON library including type definitions (cJSON struct), function declarations for all public functions, and macros for versioning and compiler compatibility.

Implements JSON Pointer navigation (cJSONUtils_GetPointer, cJSONUtils_FindPointerFromObjectTo), JSON Patch operations (cJSONUtils_ApplyPatches, cJSONUtils_GeneratePatches), JSON Merge Patch operations (cJSONUtils_MergePatch, cJSONUtils_GenerateMergePatch), and object sorting (cJSONUtils_SortObject). Uses core library functions for all underlying JSON operations.
Declares the public API for cJSON utility functions including JSON Pointer, JSON Patch, JSON Merge Patch, and sorting functions.
Demonstrates library usage by creating complex JSON structures with various types (strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, nested objects) and testing print functionality including preallocated printing.
Provides a main function that reads files from disk and passes their contents to LLVMFuzzerTestOneInput, enabling fuzzing without libFuzzer.
AFL-specific fuzzing harness that reads files, parses them with cJSON_Parse, and exercises all print functions (cJSON_Print, cJSON_PrintBuffered, cJSON_PrintUnformatted) in a loop.
Contains the LLVMFuzzerTestOneInput function that serves as the fuzzer target for cJSON parsing.

inter_module_edges.description: The utility library consumes core library functions and types to implement JSON Pointer, Patch, and Merge Patch operations. Core library's global_hooks memory management is used indirectly through core API calls.
inter_module_edges.evidence: cJSON_Utils.c directly calls core functions: cJSON_malloc, cJSON_free, cJSON_IsObject, cJSON_IsArray, cJSON_IsString, cJSON_IsNull, cJSON_Duplicate, cJSON_AddItemToObject, cJSON_AddItemToArray, cJSON_DeleteItemFromObject, cJSON_DetachItemFromObject, cJSON_GetObjectItem, cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive, cJSON_CreateArray, cJSON_CreateObject, cJSON_CreateString, cJSON_CreateNull, cJSON_Delete, cJSON_ArrayForEach, cJSON_Print, cJSON_DetachItemFromArray, cJSON_AddItemToArray, cJSON_DetachItemFromObjectCaseSensitive, cJSON_DeleteItemFromObjectCaseSensitive, cJSON_DeleteItemFromArray. The utility library reads core library's type constants (cJSON_Number, cJSON_String, cJSON_Array, cJSON_Object, cJSON_Invalid) through the cJSON struct type.

inter_module_edges.description: Testing and fuzzing code consumes core library functions to parse JSON, create JSON structures, and serialize them back to text.
inter_module_edges.evidence: test.c calls: cJSON_AddStringToObject, cJSON_AddItemToObject, cJSON_AddFalseToObject, cJSON_CreateIntArray, cJSON_AddItemToArray, cJSON_CreateArray, cJSON_Delete, cJSON_CreateString, cJSON_CreateObject, cJSON_AddNumberToObject, cJSON_CreateStringArray, cJSON_Print, cJSON_PrintPreallocated. afl.c calls: cJSON_Parse, cJSON_Print, cJSON_PrintBuffered, cJSON_PrintUnformatted, cJSON_Delete. The fuzzing code passes JSON data to core library's parsing functions and exercises serialization.

intra_module_edges.description: The header file declares types, macros, and function prototypes that the implementation file defines. The header must be included before the implementation can be compiled.
intra_module_edges.evidence: cJSON.c includes "cJSON.h" as its first local include. All public functions in cJSON.c (cJSON_Parse, cJSON_Print, cJSON_Create*, etc.) are declared in cJSON.h. The cJSON struct type and related enums (cJSON_bool, cJSON_type) are defined in cJSON.h and used throughout cJSON.c.

intra_module_edges.description: The utility header declares functions that the utility implementation defines. The header must be included before the implementation can be compiled.
intra_module_edges.evidence: cJSON_Utils.c includes "cJSON_Utils.h" as its first local include. All public functions in cJSON_Utils.c (cJSONUtils_GetPointer, cJSONUtils_ApplyPatches, cJSONUtils_GeneratePatches, cJSONUtils_MergePatch, cJSONUtils_GenerateMergePatch, cJSONUtils_SortObject, etc.) are declared in cJSON_Utils.h.

intra_module_edges.description: The fuzzing harness main function calls LLVMFuzzerTestOneInput which is defined in the fuzzer target file.
intra_module_edges.evidence: fuzz_main.c's main function calls LLVMFuzzerTestOneInput(data, size) after reading file contents. LLVMFuzzerTestOneInput is declared (has_body: false) in fuzz_main.c but its implementation is in cjson_read_fuzzer.c.

intra_module_edges.description: The AFL fuzzing harness uses the core library directly for parsing and printing, but the fuzzer target (LLVMFuzzerTestOneInput) from cjson_read_fuzzer.c represents the standard fuzzing entry point that AFL would typically call.
intra_module_edges.evidence: afl.c includes "../cJSON.h" and calls cJSON_Parse, cJSON_Print, cJSON_PrintBuffered, cJSON_PrintUnformatted, cJSON_Delete. While afl.c doesn't directly call LLVMFuzzerTestOneInput, it follows the same pattern of parsing JSON input and exercising library functions, making it part of the same fuzzing workflow.
